payload.answer: Recall selected mem_new because it is the latest surviving memory for Sam in the Goldman context. mem_old was filtered out because it is superseded by mem_new, and superseded memories are not returned as current recall results. This preserves the current state while avoiding presentation of outdated information.
payload.decision: Recall returned mem_new as the current durable memory for Sam from Goldman and filtered mem_old because mem_old was marked or inferred as superseded by the newer conflicting memory about the same entity/fact.

entity_resolution.reason: Both memory cards refer to the same person, so recall resolved them to one entity and kept only the non-superseded current fact.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new is the active memory card for Sam associated with Goldman.
mem_old was excluded from recall because it is superseded and should not be presented as current.

details: Resolved recall target to Sam in the Goldman context.
Detected that mem_old and mem_new are in conflict on the same subject/context.
Applied supersession rule: return current memory, suppress superseded memory.
Produced explanation without presenting mem_old as current.

repair_options: If mem_old should still be visible, mark it as historical instead of superseded.
If mem_new does not fully replace mem_old, split them into non-conflicting scoped memories.
If the supersession link is incorrect, remove or revise the conflict resolution metadata between mem_old and mem_new.

raw_text: {"intent":"explain_recall_decision","decision":"Recall returned mem_new as the current durable memory for Sam from Goldman and filtered mem_old because mem_old was marked or inferred as superseded by the newer conflicting memory about the same entity/fact.","memory_cards":[{"kind":"current_memory","statement":"mem_new is the active memory card for Sam associated with Goldman.","confidence":"high","entities":[{"name":"Sam","type":"person","role":"subject"},{"name":"Goldman","type":"organization","role":"context"}],"relationships":[{"subject":"mem_new","predicate":"about","object":"Sam"},{"subject":"mem_new","predicate":"associated_with","object":"Goldman"}]},{"kind":"superseded_memory","statement":"mem_old was excluded from recall because it is superseded and should not be presented as current.","confidence":"high","entities":[{"name":"Sam","type":"person","role":"subject"},{"name":"Goldman","type":"organization","role":"context"}],"relationships":[{"subject":"mem_old","predicate":"about","object":"Sam"},{"subject":"mem_old","predicate":"associated_with","object":"Goldman"},{"subject":"mem_old","predicate":"superseded_by","object":"mem_new"}]}],"entity_resolution":{"action":"reuse_existing","entity_id":"Sam","reason":"Both memory cards refer to the same person, so recall resolved them to one entity and kept only the non-superseded current fact."},"conflict_classification":"supersession","answer":"Recall selected mem_new because it is the latest surviving memory for Sam in the Goldman context. mem_old was filtered out because it is superseded by mem_new, and superseded memories are not returned as current recall results. This preserves the current state while avoiding presentation of outdated information.","citations":["mem_new","mem_old"],"repair_options":["If mem_old should still be visible, mark it as historical instead of superseded.","If mem_new does not fully replace mem_old, split them into non-conflicting scoped memories.","If the supersession link is incorrect, remove or revise the conflict resolution metadata between mem_old and mem_new."],"receipt":{"success":true,"details":["Resolved recall target to Sam in the Goldman context.","Detected that mem_old and mem_new are in conflict on the same subject/context.","Applied supersession rule: return current memory, suppress superseded memory.","Produced explanation without presenting mem_old as current."]}}
